title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Authentic Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani)
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy, step-by-step recipe. Get creamy, flavorful results at home for a perfect lunch or dinner.
longTailKeywords: Butter Chicken recipe step by step
lowCompetitionKeywords: homemade Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani recipe
restaurant style Butter Chicken at home
seo.h1: Authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?

seo.imageAlt: A bowl of creamy, authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich and creamy flavors of homemade Butter Chicken, a classic Indian delight perfect for any meal.

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a beloved Indian dish featuring tender chicken simmered in a rich, creamy, and mildly spiced tomato-based sauce. Its signature velvety texture and subtly sweet flavor make it a global favorite, perfect for a comforting and satisfying meal. This recipe guides you through creating an authentic restaurant-style experience right in your own kitchen.

ingredients.notes: or Kashmiri chili powder for color
soaked in hot water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, red chili powder, turmeric powder, 1/2 teaspoon garam masala, and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Mix well to coat the chicken. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Sear the Marinated Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon butter and the vegetable oil in a large pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook for 3-4 minutes per side, until lightly browned and partially cooked. Remove the chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauce Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add 1 tablespoon more butter. Add the finely chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the remaining 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant. Add the crushed tomatoes, remaining 1/2 teaspoon red chili powder, and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Bring to a simmer.

instructions.title: Simmer and Blend the Sauce
instructions.text: Add the soaked cashews to the simmering tomato mixture. Cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens slightly and the raw tomato smell disappears. Remove from heat and carefully transfer the mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. You may need to add a splash of water if it is too thick.

instructions.title: Combine Chicken and Sauce
instructions.text: Pour the blended sauce back into the pan. Stir in the seared chicken pieces. Add the remaining 1/2 teaspoon garam masala and sugar. Bring the curry to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat. Cook for 10-15 minutes, allowing the chicken to cook through and absorb the flavors of the sauce.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the heavy cream and the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter. Cook for another 2-3 minutes, just until heated through; do not boil after adding cream. Crush the kasoori methi between your palms and add to the curry. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro and serve hot with naan, roti, or basmati rice.

tips: For a truly smooth sauce, pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve after blending to remove any fibrous bits.
Kashmiri red chili powder provides a beautiful deep red color without excessive heat. If unavailable, use paprika for color and a pinch of cayenne for heat.
Butter Chicken tastes even better the next day! Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Reheat gently on the stovetop.
article:
Butter Chicken, also known as Murgh Makhani, stands as a crowning jewel in the vast and vibrant tapestry of Indian cuisine. Renowned globally for its rich, creamy texture and mildly spiced tomato-based sauce, it’s a dish that evokes comfort and indulgence with every spoonful. While often enjoyed for dinner, its comforting flavors and satisfying blend of protein and sauce make it an absolutely ideal choice for a luxurious Indian lunch recipe. Imagine a workday brightened by tender chicken swimming in a velvety, aromatic gravy – it’s a culinary experience that transcends the ordinary. This article will guide you through the delightful world of Murgh Makhani, revealing its origins, essential ingredients, step-by-step preparation, and tips to perfect this beloved classic.

## What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?
Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, translates literally to "chicken with butter," and this name perfectly encapsulates the essence of the dish. Originating in the 1950s at the Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi, India, it was created by Kundan Lal Jaggi. The story goes that leftover tandoori chicken pieces were simmered in a rich tomato gravy enriched with butter and cream, giving birth to a legend. The hallmark of Murgh Makhani is its smooth, slightly sweet, and tangy tomato gravy, which is generously flavored with aromatic spices like garam masala, kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), and often a hint of ginger and garlic. The chicken, traditionally tandoor-cooked, is incredibly tender, soaking up all the delicious flavors of the sauce. Its mild spice profile and luxurious texture make it universally appealing, a perfect introduction to Indian flavors for newcomers, and a cherished favorite for connoisseurs.

## Ingredients You Need for Butter Chicken
To make authentic Butter Chicken at home, you'll need a combination of fresh produce, spices, and dairy. Here's a breakdown:

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken (thighs or breast), cut into bite-sized pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 tsp red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color, less heat)
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1 tsp garam masala
*   1 tbsp lemon juice
*   Salt to taste

For the Sauce:
*   2 tbsp ghee or unsalted butter
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 (400g) can crushed tomatoes or 3-4 fresh ripe tomatoes, pureed
*   1/4 cup raw cashews (soaked in warm water for 15-20 minutes, optional but recommended for creaminess)
*   1 tsp red chili powder
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1 tsp garam masala
*   1 tsp kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   1/2 cup heavy cream
*   1-2 tsp sugar (to balance acidity)
*   Salt to taste
*   Optional: A touch of red food coloring for authentic restaurant-style color (use sparingly)

For Garnish:
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped
*   A swirl of heavy cream

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Butter Chicken
Creating this iconic dish at home is simpler than you might think. Follow these steps to create your own delicious Butter Chicken:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well, ensuring each piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, or even overnight for maximum flavor.

2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Preheat a grill, broiler, or a heavy-bottomed pan with a little oil over medium-high heat. Cook the marinated chicken until it's lightly charred on the outside and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per side, depending on the size of the pieces. Set aside.

3.  **Prepare the Sauce Base:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, melt the ghee or butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onions and sauté until they turn soft and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

4.  **Build the Sauce Flavors:** Stir in the crushed tomatoes (or puree), soaked cashews (if using), red chili powder, turmeric powder, and garam masala. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 10-15 minutes, allowing the tomatoes to break down and the spices to meld. The mixture should thicken and the oil should start to separate from the sides.

5.  **Blend the Sauce:** Carefully transfer the sauce mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky texture, pass the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pot, pressing down on any solids. This step is optional but highly recommended for restaurant-quality smoothness.

6.  **Finish the Sauce and Combine:** Return the smooth sauce to the pot over medium-low heat. Add the kasuri methi (crushed between your palms to release aroma), heavy cream, and sugar. Stir well. Bring to a gentle simmer.

7.  **Add Chicken and Simmer:** Gently fold in the cooked chicken pieces into the sauce. Simmer for another 5-10 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the rich flavors of the sauce. Taste and adjust salt and sugar as needed. If using, add a tiny drop of red food coloring for a vibrant hue.

8.  **Garnish and Serve:** Ladle the hot Butter Chicken into serving bowls. Garnish generously with fresh cilantro and a swirl of heavy cream.

## Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken
Achieving restaurant-quality Butter Chicken at home is within reach with a few expert tips:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight marination yields the best results.
*   **Charred Chicken:** While pan-frying works, grilling or broiling the chicken until it has slightly charred edges adds a smoky depth that is characteristic of authentic Murgh Makhani, mimicking the tandoor flavor.
*   **Blend Until Silky Smooth:** The texture of the sauce is paramount. Blend the sauce base thoroughly and, if possible, strain it. This removes any onion or cashew bits, resulting in an incredibly smooth and luxurious gravy.
*   **Don't Skip Kasuri Methi:** Dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) are non-negotiable for authentic flavor. Toast them lightly in a dry pan for a few seconds before crushing and adding to the sauce to enhance their aroma.
*   **Balance Flavors:** Butter Chicken is all about balance. The sugar helps cut the acidity of the tomatoes, while salt enhances all the flavors. Taste and adjust these elements to your preference. A little extra butter at the end can also boost richness.

## Serving Suggestions for Murgh Makhani
Enjoy your homemade Butter Chicken with an array of traditional Indian accompaniments to complete the meal. The most classic pairing is with warm, fluffy naan bread – especially garlic naan or butter naan – perfect for scooping up every last drop of the rich gravy. Alternatively, serve it alongside fragrant Basmati rice or jeera rice (cumin rice) for a comforting and satisfying lunch. To cut through the richness, a side of cool, refreshing raita (yogurt with cucumber and spices) or a simple kachumber salad (diced onion, tomato, cucumber) provides a lovely contrast. A small bowl of Indian pickle (achaar) or mango chutney can also add an exciting tang or sweetness to each bite.

## Variations and Substitutions for Butter Chicken
While classic Butter Chicken is a staple, don't hesitate to experiment with variations and substitutions to suit dietary needs or personal preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Alternatives:** For a meat-free version, replace chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese) to make Paneer Makhani, or use firm tofu, cauliflower, or mixed vegetables like potatoes and peas for a delicious vegetarian Murgh Makhani experience.
*   **Vegan Option:** To make this dish vegan, substitute ghee and butter with a neutral oil, and replace heavy cream with full-fat coconut milk or a cashew cream blended from soaked cashews and water. Ensure your yogurt for marination (if making a chicken substitute) is plant-based.
*   **Spice Level Adjustment:** If you prefer more heat, increase the amount of red chili powder or add a few slit green chilies to the sauce while it simmers. For less heat, reduce the chili powder or use a milder variety like paprika.
*   **Healthier Twist:** For a slightly lighter version, reduce the amount of butter and cream. You can also substitute some of the heavy cream with Greek yogurt (added at the very end to prevent curdling) for a tangy, lower-fat option.
*   **Nut-Free Version:** If you have a nut allergy, you can omit the cashews from the sauce. To maintain some thickness, you can add a tablespoon of cornstarch slurry (cornstarch mixed with a little water) at the end, or use a small amount of roasted poppy seeds or sunflower seeds, blended into the sauce.

This iconic Butter Chicken recipe is more than just a meal; it's an experience of comfort, flavor, and tradition. Perfect for a hearty lunch or a special dinner, its universally appealing taste makes it a dish everyone will love. Embrace the joy of cooking and bring the magic of Murgh Makhani into your kitchen today!
